sections.heading: Overview
sections.content:
Global FX Overview — Asian Session

The dollar extends its consolidation phase during Asian trading as markets digest mixed signals from weekend G7 communications and position ahead of key US data releases. Risk sentiment remains cautiously constructive with Asian equities posting modest gains, though underlying concerns about global growth momentum continue to weigh on sentiment. The greenback's modest strength reflects defensive positioning rather than conviction buying.

Currency Strength Rankings
Strongest to weakest intraday: AUD (+0.09%), USD/CHF (+0.21%), USD/JPY (+0.05%), USD/CAD (+0.08%), EUR/USD (-0.09%), GBP/USD (-0.05%), NZD/USD (-0.41%). Commodity currencies show divergence with AUD benefiting from iron ore price stability while NZD faces headwinds from dovish RBNZ expectations. Dollar strength is broad-based but lacks conviction, suggesting consolidation rather than breakout momentum.

FX Majors Analysis

EUR/USD trades at 1.1632, down 0.09% intraday but holding above key support at 1.1620. The pair remains range-bound between 1.1600-1.1680 ahead of Thursday's ECB minutes release. Bias remains neutral with a slight bearish tilt on dollar strength. Watch for break below 1.1620 to target 1.1580.

GBP/

sections.heading: Analysis
sections.content:
USD at 1.3475 shows resilience despite 0.05% intraday decline. Cable continues to benefit from sticky UK inflation expectations and BoE hawkish positioning. Weekly gains of 0.59% reflect underlying sterling strength. Support at 1.3450 remains intact with bias neutral to bullish on any dips below 1.3460.

USD/JPY holds steady at 159.0300, up 0.05% intraday near critical intervention zone. BoJ verbal warnings intensify above 159.00 with markets pricing 65% probability of intervention above 160.00. Bias remains cautiously bullish but risk/reward unfavorable at current levels. Key resistance 159.50-160.00.

AUD/USD leads G10 performance at 0.7164, up 0.09% intraday and 0.81% weekly. RBA hawkish stance and Chinese stimulus hopes underpin aussie strength. Break above 0.7180 targets 0.7220. Bias bullish on China reopening optimism and carry appeal.

USD/CHF advances 0.21% to 0.7839 as safe-haven demand moderates. SNB intervention concerns remain subdued below 0.7850. Weekly decline of 0.62% reflects broader dollar weakness. Bias neutral with upside capped at 0.7850.

USD/CAD gains 0.08% to 1.3813 despite oil price stability. BoC dovish pivot weighs on loonie with rate cut expectations building. Break above 1.3820 opens

sections.heading: Outlook
sections.content:
1.3850. Bias mildly bullish on divergent monetary policy expectations.

NZD/USD weakest major, down 0.41% to 0.5849 on RBNZ dovish tilt expectations. Dairy price weakness and China growth concerns pressure kiwi. Support at 0.5840 critical; break targets 0.5810. Bias bearish on fundamental deterioration.

FX Crosses Rotation

EUR/JPY at 184.9440 consolidates near resistance with ECB/BoJ divergence theme intact. GBP/JPY at 214.3000 benefits from BoE hawkishness versus BoJ accommodation. AUD/JPY 113.8910 leads crosses on carry appeal. EUR/GBP 0.8630 reflects BoE/ECB policy divergence with sterling preferred.

Flows and Positioning
• Carry trade demand supports AUD/JPY, NZD/JPY despite vol concerns
• Corporate month-end flows favor USD strength into London session
• Systematic trend followers remain long USD against EUR, short JPY crosses
• Real money showing renewed interest in GBP on inflation persistence

Trade Ideas Intraday

LONG: AUD/USD above 0.7150 targeting 0.7200 on China optimism; GBP/JPY above 214.00 targeting 215.50 on BoE/BoJ divergence; USD/CAD above 1.3810 targeting 1.3850 on BoC dovishness.

SHORT: NZD/USD below 0.5850 targeting 0.5810 on RBNZ dovish shift; EUR/GBP above 0.8640 targeting 0.8600 on Bo
